get.description: List and filter all unified content (topics). Optionally apply some filtering/sorting to find topics matching specific criteria. It's possible to list unified content from all categories, or filtered on specific categories. Only the first 10,000 topics matching the filter can be queried, requests for further topics will return a 422 error. *IMPORTANT NOTES* : 1) using parameters like categoryId and categoryIds simultaneously leads to their merging 2) using categoryId(s) and productAreaIds simultaneously gives OR effect

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The search term which will match the title, content. Skipping this parameter will return all topics

get.summary: List unified content (topics) for a category
get.description: List and filter unified content (topics) for a specific category. Optionally apply some filtering/sorting to find topics matching specific criteria inside this category

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the category to fetch topics for

get.summary: Search by term across unified content
get.description: Perform a full text search on unified topics by passing a search term in the `q` parameter. The search will be performed against topic `title` and `content` fields. It's possible to specify additional optional filters to further limit the results. By default this endpoint will only return visible topics

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The search term which will match the title, content. Skipping this parameter will return all topics

parameters.name: trashed
parameters.description: Filter the search results by trashed status, for example to retrieve only trashed topics. Not passing this parameter will return only visible topics

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of category ids. Not passing this parameter will return topics from all categories

parameters.name: contentTypes
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of content types

post.description:
A moderator can create an article in a category, providing the title and content.

An article is always created as a draft (which later can be published) and open (authors can reply to it). Moderators can optionally add public labels to provide more context about the type of article. It can also optionally have a featured image with a valid url. The allowed image extensions for featured image are `png`, `jpeg`, `jpg`, `gif`.
Moderators can optionally create an article with a poll. A poll consists of a title and options. Existing authors can vote on the poll attached to an article. Authors can like (& unlike!) an article and individual replies to the article.

Moderators can optionally create an article as closed, which means that only moderators can reply to it. Moderators can also optionally create an article sticky, which highlights the article at the top of the category on the community.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of the author of the article

parameters.name: moderatorId
parameters.description: The ID of the moderator who will create the article on behalf of the author (who in this case can be a registered user as well)

id.description: The private ID of the created article

publicId.description: The public ID of the created article

post.description: A valid `moderatorId` value is required to publish an article.

parameters.name: moderatorId
parameters.description: ID of the moderator publishing the article

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of the author of this reply

parameters.name: likedBy
parameters.description: ID the author giving the like

parameters.name: unlikedBy
parameters.description: ID the author revoking the like

204.description: Like was revoked from the discussion

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: ID of the author or moderator making the edits

get.description: Fetches a paginated list of articles sorted by last activity in descending order. Last activity is the time the article was last replied.

Fetches a paginated list of articles in a category. The category must be a public category. The result is sorted by last activity in descending order. Last activity is the time the article was last replied.

get.summary: Get visible topics count per category
get.description: Returns the count of visible (non-spam, non-trashed, non-pending, non-draft) topics per category. This includes questions, conversations, ideas, articles, and product updates. Note: This endpoint does not return counts for sections.

result.description: Object mapping category IDs to their visible topics count

additionalProperties.description: Number of visible topics in the category

post.description:
Authors can start a conversation in a category, providing the title and content of the conversation.

A conversation started by an author is always open, meaning the original author and other authors can reply to it. Authors can also like (& unlike!) a conversation and individual replies within the conversation. Authors can also optionally add public tags when starting a conversation. Authors can optionally start a conversation with a poll. A poll consists of a title and options. Existing authors can vote on the poll attached to conversations.

Moderators can optionally start a conversation as closed, which means that only moderators can reply to it. Moderators can also optionally start a conversation as sticky, which highlights the conversation at the top of the category on the community.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of author of the conversation

id.description: The private ID of the created conversation

publicId.description: The public ID of the created conversation

parameters.name: likedBy
parameters.description: ID of the author giving the like

parameters.name: unlikedBy
parameters.description: ID of the author revoking the like

204.description: Like was revoked from the discussion

post.summary: Edit the opening post content of a conversation
post.description:
An author can change the content of a conversation for a limited time after starting the conversation. The default edit time span is 60 minutes.
Moderators can always change the content of a conversation that was started by any other author.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: ID of the author or moderator making the edits

post.description:
Authors can submit an idea, providing the title and content of the idea.

A submitted idea by an author is always open, meaning the original author and other authors can reply to it. The author's vote is added automatically and they also can unvote their ideas. Authors can also optionally add public tags and product areas when submitting an idea.

Moderators can optionally submit an idea as closed, which means that only moderators can reply to it.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of author of the idea

Is-Spam.description: 1 if the idea is spam, 0 if not spam

Is-Pending.description: 1 if the idea is pending, 0 if not pending

id.description: The private ID of the created idea

publicId.description: The public ID of the created idea

parameters.name: votedBy
parameters.description: ID of the author giving the vote

parameters.name: unvotedBy
parameters.description: ID of the author revoking the vote

204.description: Vote was revoked from the idea

Moderators can always change the content of an idea that was submitted by any other author.

post.description:
A moderator can create a productUpdate, providing the title and content.

An productUpdate is always created as a draft (which later can be published) and open (authors can reply to it). Moderators can optionally add public labels to provide more context about the type of productUpdate. It can also optionally have a featured image with a valid url. The allowed image extensions for featured image are `png`, `jpeg`, `jpg`, `gif`.
Moderators can optionally create a productUpdate with a poll. A poll consists of a title and options. Existing authors can vote on the poll attached to a productUpdate. Authors can like (& unlike!) a productUpdate and individual replies to the productUpdate.

Moderators can optionally create a productUpdate as closed, which means that only moderators can reply to it.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of the author of the productUpdate

parameters.name: moderatorId
parameters.description: The ID of the moderator who will create the product update on behalf of the author (who in this case can be a registered user as well)

id.description: The private ID of the created product update

publicId.description: The public ID of the created product update

post.description: A valid `moderatorId` value is required to publish a productUpdate.

parameters.name: moderatorId
parameters.description: ID of the moderator publishing the productUpdate

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of the author of this reply

Is-Spam.description: 1 if the reply is spam, 0 if not spam

Is-Pending.description: 1 if the reply is pending, 0 if not pending

parameters.name: likedBy
parameters.description: ID the author giving the like

parameters.name: unlikedBy
parameters.description: ID the author revoking the like

204.description: Like was revoked from the discussion

204.description: Question reply marked as not spam

post.description:
An author can ask a question in a category, providing the title and content.

A question asked by an author is always open, meaning the original author and other authors can reply to it. Authors can also like (& unlike!) a question and individual replies to the question. Authors can also optionally add public tags when asking a question. Authors can optionally ask a question with a poll. A poll consists of a title and options. Existing authors can vote on the poll attached to the question.

Moderators can optionally ask a question as closed, which means that only moderators can reply to it. Moderators can also optionally ask a question as sticky, which highlights the question at the top of the category on the community.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of the author of the question

id.description: The private ID of the created question

publicId.description: The public ID of the created question

post.summary: Mark a reply to a question as the answer
post.description:
The author who originally asked the question or any moderator can mark a reply that resolves the question as answer.
A question can only have one answer. Note, as the question receives more replies the answer can be updated by the author (or any moderator) who considers another reply to be a more suitable answer.

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: ID of the author or moderator marking the answer

get.description: Returns a hierarchical tree structure of categories filtered by module(s). The response is organized by category module, with each category containing its nested children recursively. Categories are sorted by displayOrder. If topLevelSectionIds is provided, only the specified top-level sections are returned (this filter is only available when querying a single category module).

parameters.name: module
parameters.description: Array of category modules to retrieve. Valid values: community, knowledge-base, groups. Example: ?module[]=community&module[]=groups

parameters.name: topLevelSectionIds
parameters.description: Optional array of top-level section IDs to filter by. Only available when querying a single module. Example: ?topLevelSectionIds[]=1&topLevelSectionIds[]=2

parameters.name: authorId
parameters.description: The ID of the author making the request

result.description: Object keyed by category module, containing arrays of category trees

additionalProperties.description: Array of top-level categories for this module

type.description: Category module identifier (community, knowledge-base, or groups)

parentId.description: Parent category ID, null for top-level categories

isSection.description: Whether this category is a container/section (renamed from isContainer)

children.description: Nested array of child categories with the same structure

self.description: The API urls of the requested object

role_arn.description: Customer IAM role ARN. Required when auth_type is aws_sigv4.

external_id.description: External ID for STS AssumeRole. Required when auth_type is aws_sigv4.

region.description: AWS region of the target endpoint. Required when auth_type is aws_sigv4.

service.description: AWS service for signing scope. Optional, defaults to execute-api.

example.description: Category is not accessible for this user

contentType.description: Refers to the the type of content this topic was projected from

id.description: The original ID of the content type this topic was based on. This ID can be used on the endpoints belonging to this content-type to fetch the original data

content.example: This is the content of a conversation

featuredImage.description: **Note:** applies to **article** type only, other content-types will always have an empty string value. This value can be an absolute URL to the image featured for this article, or an empty string if no image was provided

publicLabel.description: **Note:** applies to **article** type only, other content-types will always have an empty string value. This value contains a user-defined label which will be shown to the public on the community

tags.description: A list of public tags assigned to the original content-type. These tags are shown to the public on the community

moderatorTags.description: A list of moderator tags assigned to the original content-type. These tags are not shown to the public on the community. These tags can only be set by moderators in the Control environment

moderationLabel.description: A moderation label assigned to the original content-type. This label is not shown to the public on the community. This label can only be set by moderators in the Control environment

replyCount.description: The total number of visible replies

voteSet.description: A list of user ids that voted for this idea

trashed.description: A flag indicating whether or not the original content-type was trashed. Trashed means not visible to the public on the community. If this value is `false` it means the content was visible

sticky.description: A flag indicating whether or not the original content-type was made sticky on the community. Sticky topics will always appear at the top of the overview pages on the community

bestAnswer.description: A flag indicating if there is a best answer

lastContributor.description: Representation of the author that replied most recently to this topic

lastActivityAt.description: Date indicating when the original content-type received it's latest reply

productAreas.description: A list of product areas assigned to the original content-type

ReplySort.name: sort
ReplySort.description:
Defines the field to sort the results on. By default the response is sorted by oldest first.
  • oldestFirst : The least recent reply to the most recent reply.
  • mostRecentFirst : Ordered by most recent to least recent.
  • mostLiked : Ordered by replies which have the most likes to the least likes.

PageNumber.name: page
PageNumber.description: Selects the page to retrieve on paginated responses. Used in combination with the `pageSize` parameter to paginate over results

PageSize.name: pageSize
PageSize.description: Limits the number of items returned in a single paginated response. Used in combination with the `page` parameter to paginate over results

TopicCategoryId.name: categoryId
TopicCategoryId.description: Deprecated it's recommended to use categoryIds

TopicProductAreaIds.name: productAreaIds[]
TopicProductAreaIds.description: A list of product area ids

ContentType.name: contentType
ContentType.description: Deprecated, it's recommended to use contentTypes

TopicContentTypes.name: contentTypes[]
TopicContentTypes.description: A list of topic content types

TopicTags.name: tags
TopicTags.description: A comma-separated list of public tags

TopicModeratorTags.name: moderatorTags
TopicModeratorTags.description: A comma-separated list of moderator tags

TopicModerationLabels.name: moderationLabels
TopicModerationLabels.description: A comma-separated list of moderation labels

TopicCreatedAt.name: createdAt
TopicCreatedAt.description: A date range to filter topics based on creation date

TopicLastActivity.name: lastActivityAt
TopicLastActivity.description: A date range to filter topics based on last activity

TopicSort.name: sort
TopicSort.description: Defines the field to sort the results on. The sort order will be descending. By default the results are ordered by recent activity from most recent to least recent

PathArticleId.name: id
PathArticleId.description: ID of the article to interact with

PathArticleReplyId.name: id
PathArticleReplyId.description: ID of the article reply to interact with

PathProductUpdateId.name: id
PathProductUpdateId.description: ID of the product update to interact with

PathProductUpdateReplyId.name: id
PathProductUpdateReplyId.description: ID of the product update reply to interact with

PathIdeaId.name: id
PathIdeaId.description: ID of the idea to interact with

PathIdeaReplyId.name: id
PathIdeaReplyId.description: ID of the idea reply to interact with

PathIdeaStatusId.name: id
PathIdeaStatusId.description: ID of the idea status to interact with

PathConversationId.name: id
PathConversationId.description: ID of the conversation to interact with

PathConversationReplyId.name: id
PathConversationReplyId.description: ID of the conversation reply to interact with

PathQuestionId.name: id
PathQuestionId.description: ID of the question to interact with

PathQuestionReplyId.name: replyId
PathQuestionReplyId.description: ID of the question reply to interact with

tags.name: Articles
tags.description:
Articles enable moderators to share rich content with the community. Articles can only be created by Moderators. Articles are created as drafts and can then be published (made publicly visible) separately.

You can also add other rich elements to articles, including a featured image (displayed as a hero/thumbnail image on the community), and a public label (a description of the purpose/intent of the article, e.g. News, Update, Knowledge Base).

tags.name: Conversations
tags.description: Conversations enable users to start a conversation around a particular subject with the community by sharing content.

tags.name: Questions
tags.description:
A user can ask a question about a particular subject to the community. Users can reply to the question.

The author who originally asked the question or any moderator can mark the reply that resolves the question as best answer. A question can only have one best answer. Note, the best answer to the question can be updated as the question receives more replies and the author (or any moderator) considers another reply to be a better answer.

tags.name: Unified content (Topics)
tags.description:
You can use these endpoints to build lists containing different content types (articles, conversations or questions).

A topic is a read-only version of any article/conversation/question created in the community. A topic contains all the attributes shared between the content types. There are no specific behaviours for topics.

The attributes are projected in a unified way, regardless of the content type it originates from.  You can easily display lists of content created in the platform regardless of the differences between the content types.

Topics endpoints have additional filtering and search functionality. For example, you can search for topics containing specific search terms across all content types; you can then use the response to retrieve specific data directly from the article/conversation/question endpoints.

tags.name: Public Tags
tags.description: Public tags are a way to help users on the community find relevant topics grouped around a subject that spans across different categories.
